title: How to Set Boundaries Around Tinnitus Conversations
phrases: "There's a meaningful difference between self-improvement and self-understanding. One adds. The other reveals."
"Information without integration is just intellectual hoarding."
"We are not our thoughts, but we are responsible for our relationship to them."
"You don't arrive at peace. You stop walking away from it."
researchers: Alan Watts (Eastern philosophy for Western minds)

title: The Role of Hope in Tinnitus Recovery Without False Promises
phrases: "The mind is not the enemy. The identification with it is."
"What we call 'the present moment' is not a place you go. It's the only place you've ever been."
"The algorithm of your attention determines the landscape of your experience."
"There is no version of growth that doesn't involve the dissolution of something you thought was permanent."
"Your nervous system doesn't care about your philosophy. It cares about what happened at three years old."
interjections: I get it. Really, I do.
researchers: Pawel Jastreboff (TRT creator, neurophysiological model of tinnitus)
Sam Harris (secular meditation, neuroscience of consciousness)

title: Building Post-Traumatic Growth from Tinnitus Experience
phrases: "The most important things in life cannot be understood - only experienced."

title: The Psychology of Habituation What Actually Happens
phrases: "Stillness is not something you achieve. It's what's already here beneath the achieving."
"The wellness industry sells solutions to problems it helps you believe you have."
"The body remembers what the mind would prefer to file away."
"You cannot think your way into a felt sense of safety. The body has its own logic."
existingBody:
The Subtle Art of Habituation: An Invitation to Understanding

In the quiet corridors of our everyday existence, there is a remarkable, yet often overlooked, process unfolding—habituation.
